message.content:
{
  "lecture_id": "linear_algebra_002",
  "knowledge_objects": [
    {
      "id": "inner_product",
      "name": "Inner Product",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "An operation that takes two vectors and returns a scalar.",
      "source_span": "An inner product is an operation that takes two vectors and returns a scalar."
    },
    {
      "id": "dot_product",
      "name": "Dot Product",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": ["standard inner product"],
      "short_definition": "The standard inner product in Euclidean space.",
      "source_span": "In Euclidean space, the standard inner product is the dot product."
    },
    {
      "id": "orthogonal_vectors",
      "name": "Orthogonal Vectors",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Two vectors are orthogonal when their inner product is zero.",
      "source_span": "Two vectors are orthogonal when their inner product is zero."
    },
    {
      "id": "orthogonal_projection",
      "name": "Orthogonal Projection",
      "type": "Method",
      "aliases": ["projection"],
      "short_definition": "A method for finding the closest point in a subspace to a given vector.",
      "source_span": "Orthogonal projection is a method for finding the closest point in a subspace to a given vector."
    },
    {
      "id": "projection_formula",
      "name": "Projection Formula",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The projection of v onto the subspace spanned by u is (v·u)/(u·u) u.",
      "source_span": "proj_u(v) = (v·u)/(u·u) u"
    },
    {
      "id": "least_squares_problem",
      "name": "Least Squares Problem",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A problem that uses projection ideas to solve inconsistent systems by minimizing ||Ax-b||^2.",
      "source_span": "Least squares problems use projection ideas to solve inconsistent systems."
    },
    {
      "id": "normal_equations",
      "name": "Normal Equations",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The equations A^T A x = A^T b that the solution to a least squares problem satisfies when columns of A are independent.",
      "source_span": "A^T A x = A^T b"
    }
  ]
}
